header_comments: Grace period to keep resolved entries for late awaitDecision calls
inline_comments: Caller metadata (best-effort). Used to prevent other clients from replaying an approval id.

Register an approval record and return a promise that resolves when the decision is made. This separates registration (synchronous) from waiting (async), allowing callers to confirm registration before the decision is made.
Idempotent: return existing promise if still pending
Create entry first so we can capture it in the closure (not re-fetch from map)
@deprecated Use register() instead for explicit separation of registration and waiting.
Prevent double-resolve (e.g., if called after timeout already resolved)
Resolve the promise first, then delete after a grace period.
This allows in-flight awaitDecision calls to find the resolved entry.
Only delete if the entry hasn't been replaced
One-time approvals must be consumed atomically so the same runId
cannot be replayed during the resolved-entry grace window.
Wait for decision on an already-registered approval. Returns the decision promise if the ID is pending, null otherwise.

concerns: Multiple concerns detected: database, api, logging
